Action: City/transit operator is only procuring zero emission buses
Short Name: Procure only zero emissions buses

Action: City has restrictions (e.g. charges or bans) on high polluting vehicles that cover a significant part of the city
Short Name: Restrictions on high polluting vehicles

Action: City is reallocating road space from cars to active and sustainable modes on a permanent basis
Short Name: Reallocation of road space to active transport

Action: City is implementing measures to improve network coverage, frequency, capacity, speed, reliability, accessibility, resilience, safety, affordability, comfort and convenience of public transport (e.g. buses, BRT, informal transport, intra-city rail.)
Short Name: Improvement of quality of public transport

Action: City is (alone or with partners) implementing the key measures to promote transport electrification
Short Name: Transport electrification

Action: City is procuring only zero-emission vehicles for the municipal fleet and specifying zero emission vehicles in municipal procurements
Short Name: Procure only zero emission municipal vehicles

Action: City is implementing a programme of action to reduce road danger, in order to ensure people walking, cycling and using public transport are safe and feel safe
Short Name: City-wide speed restrictions

Action: City has adopted transit-oriented development policies that prioritise higher-density, mixed-use growth near transit nodes, with a particular emphasis on integrating affordable housing in these areas
Short Name: Promote transit-oriented development and affordable housing

Action: City has adopted policies that promote polycentric form and proximity to services and amenities across neighbourhoods, by reversing single-use zoning and incentivising mixed-use development
Short Name: Proximity city and mixed-use development

Action: City is implementing/enforcing building energy regulations, codes or policies on a mandatory pathway to achieve net zero carbon new buildings
Short Name: Building energy regulations, codes or policies towards net zero carbon new buildings

Action: City is implementing/enforcing mandatory energy or emissions performance standards for existing buildings (on a pathway to net zero carbon)
Short Name: Mandatory energy/emissions performance standards for existing buildings

Action: City is incentivising or implementing [citywide/significant] actions towards grid decarbonization (either building-scale or large scale)
Short Name: Implement actions towards grid decarbonization

Action: City is implementing or incentivising a phase out of fossil fuel or solid fuel technologies for heating and cooking
Short Name: Phase out of fossil or solid fuel heating and cooking

Action: City is implementing renewable energy mandates for buildings
Short Name: Renewable energy mandates for buildings

Action: City is implementing policies to deliver net zero carbon municipal buildings (through retrofits and/or onsite renewable energy requirements)
Short Name: Policies towards net zero carbon municipal buildings

Action: City is implementing retrofit programs for existing, privately-owned buildings, that put the city on a pathway to net zero carbon
Short Name: Retrofit programs for private buildings

Action: City is including measures to reduce the climate impacts of the built environment (embodied carbon and climate risks of buildings and/or infrastructure assets)
Short Name: Measures to reduce embodied emissions and climate risk in buildings

Action: City has defined a baseline and set an embodied emissions reduction target for new build, major renovations and/or infrastructure
Short Name: Baseline and embodied emissions reduction target set

Action: City is implementing citywide segregated collection (food waste/organics, recycling, residual/rubbish) for majority of businesses and residences
Short Name: City-wide segregated waste collection

Action: City is restricting single use and non-recyclable materials
Short Name: Restrict single use and non-recyclable materials

Action: City has volume-based waste collection fees or incentives
Short Name: Waste collection fees or incentives (volume-based)

Action: City is achieving universal waste collection
Short Name: Universal waste collection

Action: City has a sanitary landfill (HDPE lining, landfill gas management and leachate treatment)
Short Name: Sanitary landfill

Action: City is creating/aligning school feeding programs to locally relevant low carbon diet
Short Name: School feeding programs towards low carbon diets

Action: City is regulating or activating programs for food businesses to minimise food related carbon emissions
Short Name: Food businesses programs to minimize carbon emissions

Action: City has food assistance programmes which follow locally relevant low carbon diets (eg prioritizing plant-protein)
Short Name: Food assistance programmes towards low carbon diets

Action: City has sustainable urban agriculture as part of their food security programmes (eg regenerative agriculture programs or supporting plant based proteins production)
Short Name: Food security programmes

Action: City is implementing water system efficiency improvements and identifying new sources of supply to reduce water supply risks
Short Name: Water system efficiency improvements

Action: City is implementing cool surfaces (roofs, pavements) to reduce urban heat
Short Name: Cool surfaces to reduce urban heat

Action: City is implementing Sustainable Urban Drainage Systems (SUDs)
Short Name: Sustainable urban drainage systems

Action: City has an emergency response plan in implementation to protect vulnerable citizens from extreme weather events
Short Name: Emergency Preparedness

Action: City is implementing coastal protection against sea level rise and storm surges
Short Name: Coastal protection

Action: City is implementing water conservation and water demand management solutions to increase water availability
Short Name: Water conservation and demand solutions

Action: City is increasing green spaces and urban canopy cover in vulnerable areas
Short Name: Green spaces and urban canopy cover

Action: City has adequate air quality city-wide monitoring
Short Name: Air quality city-wide monitoring

Action: City has adopted land use policies that promote increased density in appropriate areas, such as reversing single-family zoning and incentivising infill development
Short Name: Promote increased density and infill development

Action: City has adopted land use policies that limit urban sprawl and discourage development on natural land
Short Name: Limit urban sprawl and protect natural land

Action: City has adopted evidence-based spatial zoning to regulate development in areas at high risk from climate impacts—particularly flooding and landslides—and, where applicable, has upgraded climate-vulnerable informal settlements
Short Name: Risk-Informed Land Use Planning and Settlement Upgrading

Action: City is treating significant amounts of organic waste
Short Name: Organic waste treatment

Action: Port and port city (alone or with partners) is implementing measures to achieve scope 1, 2 net-zero emissions at port terminals
Short Name: Measures to achieve net zero emissions at port terminals

Action: Port or Port city is taking measures to promote zero emissions fuels for port and related operations
Short Name: Measures to develop zero emissions fuels supply chains (by port or port city)
